title: Dynamic Wallet Change Addresses to prevent UTXO Limit Failures
body:
Addresses the issue of `mempool.space` /utxo failures for high-volume wallets. This implements a dynamic change address pool of up to 200 slots, avoiding addresses approaching 420 unspent outputs. Additionally, a new feature explicitly sweeps all existing wallet balances to the change address with the lowest unspent output count to efficiently recycle funding for continuous low-value posting.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [11360931603754073358](https://jules.google.com/task/11360931603754073358) started by @embiimob*

title: Optimize mempool.space API usage by deferring /utxo calls
body:
- Modified `broadcastTx` to gather `getBalance` info for all addresses first, selecting candidates without fetching their `utxos`. UTXOs are fetched lazily via a new `fetchUtxosForSigner` helper if the address contains enough funds.
- Modified `consolidateChangeFunds` to skip calling `/utxo` for addresses with zero confirmed balance.
- Updated the table description in `README.md` to reflect the new behavior of `/utxo`.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [7276321520390599167](https://jules.google.com/task/7276321520390599167) started by @embiimob*

title: Fix HTML entity parsing in mentions and hashtags
body:
Updated regular expressions for `annotateText`, `MENTION_RE`, `TAG_RE`, and `parseHashtags` to ensure they only match mentions and tags that follow non-word characters or the start of the string, and explicitly ignore cases where they are preceded by `&` or `;` to prevent matching inside HTML entities.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [8141347623623446618](https://jules.google.com/task/8141347623623446618) started by @embiimob*

title: Removing default search terms from trending results
body: Pull request created by AI Agent

title: Refactor “For you” feed to trend-first composition with deduped community mix
body:
“For you” was effectively re-querying and re-showing the same recent results already covered by the Recent tab. This change makes For you trend-first, lightly blended with community content, while keeping Community feed behavior isolated and avoiding redundant API fanout.

- **For you data model (trend-first, not recent-first)**
  - Reworked `fetchHomeRootsPage('trending', ...)` to build For you from `GetTrendingRootSearches` + per-trend root searches.
  - Removed dependence on the default recent `searchRoots('')` path for the For you tab.

- **Light community blending**
  - Added controlled mixing of community posts into For you (approximately 4 trend items : 1 community item).
  - Preserved strict dedupe by `txId` across trend and community sources.

- **Redundant call elimination**
  - Added `S.forYouCache` with:
    - request coalescing (`reqs` map),
    - TTL-based reuse,
    - follower-set keyed invalidation.
  - Avoids repeated recomputation and duplicate API bursts while paginating/refreshing For you.

- **Cache lifecycle / invalidation**
  - Introduced `clearForYouCache()`.
  - Invalidate For you cache on follow/block mutations and brand-home reset to keep feed composition aligned with social graph changes.

- **Search semantics preserved for p2fk cache compatibility**
  - Kept existing `searchRoots` behavior that normalizes input and uses `*` for empty/default searches where relevant.

```js
if (tab === 'trending') {
  const trendRows = await getTrendingRootSearches(8);
  const trendParts = await Promise.all(trends.map(t => searchRoots(t, trendQty, 0)));
  const communityRows = key ? await refreshCommunityCache({ force: false }) : [];
  // mix ~4 trend : 1 community, dedupe by txId, cache result window
}
```

title: Normalize root search strings to avoid wildcard-framed trend keys
Update profile social actions to show immediate Followed/Blocked state
body:
Profile social actions did not reflect state changes clearly after click, so users could not immediately tell whether follow/block succeeded. This change makes follow/block state explicit and updates button UI immediately on interaction.

- **Stateful button labels on profile cards**
  - Follow button now renders `Followed` when active (instead of `Unfollow`).
  - Block button now renders `Blocked` when active (instead of `Unblock`).

- **Immediate UI state sync after toggles**
  - Added `syncProfileSocialButtons(addr)` to update text/style for visible profile social buttons without waiting for a full re-render.
  - Hooked sync into both `toggleFollowAddr` and `toggleBlockAddr`.

- **Targeted DOM binding for social actions**
  - Added `data-social-action` and `data-addr` attributes to profile action buttons so state updates apply only to relevant controls.

```html
<button
  class="btn btn-sm ${followed ? 'btn-out' : 'btn-acc'}"
  data-social-action="follow"
  data-addr="${esc(profile.addr)}"
  onclick="toggleFollowAddr('${esc(profile.addr)}')">
  ${followed ? 'Followed' : 'Follow'}
</button>
```

title: Debounce Explore search requests to 5s idle window
Replace raw API error messages with funny rate-limit quips
body:
Intermittent CORS failures caused by rate-limiting were surfaced to users as opaque `"API request failed (network/CORS)"` error strings. All feed/search error renders now show a randomly-selected funny message instead. ## Changes - **Error classification in `fetchJ`** — each thrown error is tagged with a `.kind` property: `ratelimit` (network/CORS), `timeout`, `http`, or `parse` - **`funnyErrMsg(err)`** — helper that selects a random quip from the appropriate pool based on `err.kind`; defaults to the rate-limit pool for untagged errors - **Quip pools** — four arrays of messages near constants: - `QUIPS_RATELIMIT` (20) — network/CORS failures; several reference Sup!? and all tell the user to count to 10 - `QUIPS_TIMEOUT` (5) — request timeout - `QUIPS_HTTP` (5) — server-side HTTP errors - `QUIPS_PARSE` (2) — invalid JSON - **All `feed-err` render sites updated** — home feed, community feed, explore/search, profile page, and thread view all call `funnyErrMsg(e)` instead of `esc(e.message)` ```js // Before feed.innerHTML = `
Could not load feed: ${esc(e.message)}
`; // After feed.innerHTML = `
${funnyErrMsg(e)}
`; // e.g. "Sup!? is giving you the hand. 🤚 Chill for a sec and try again." ```

title: Ensure `GetKnownRootsBySearchString` always uses wildcard search strings
body:
Some `GetKnownRootsBySearchString` calls were sent without `*` in `searchString`, missing the API cache path and causing multi-second response times. This change standardizes root-search queries to always include wildcard semantics.

- **Root search request normalization**
  - Updated `searchRoots(...)` to enforce wildcard formatting before calling `GetKnownRootsBySearchString`:
    - empty query → `*`
    - plain query → `*<query>*`
    - existing wildcard query → preserved
  - This keeps cached broad-search behavior consistent across home/explore/search call paths that share `searchRoots(...)`.

- **Docs alignment**
  - Updated API usage docs to reflect wildcard-based root search (`searchString=*<q>*`) and explicit `searchString=*` behavior for broad feed queries.

```js
const raw = norm(q).trim();
const searchString = raw ? (raw.includes('*') ? raw : `*${raw}*`) : '*';
return fetchJ(`${API_BASE}/GetKnownRootsBySearchString?searchString=${encodeURIComponent(searchString)}&qty=${qty}&skip=${Math.max(0,Number(skip)||0)}&mainnet=false&showSystemFiles=false`, 20000);
```

Add Compose file upload flow for ephemeral IPFS attachments via p2fk.io
body:
This change adds native file uploads in Compose using `POST /ipfs` on `p2fk.io` while preserving the existing manual IPFS/link attachment input and Sup!? URN normalization behavior. Uploaded files are normalized to `IPFS:CID/filename.ext` and preview readiness is validated against the public `ipfs.io` gateway.

- **Compose upload entrypoint**
  - Added an `Upload` action in the compose attachment controls.
  - Kept current text input + `＋ Attach` flow unchanged for manual IPFS/on-chain/URL references.

- **IPFS upload + normalization**
  - Added browser upload handling with `FormData` to `https://p2fk.io/ipfs`.
  - Extracts CID from varied response shapes and canonicalizes attachment tokens to:
    - `IPFS:<cid>/<sanitized-filename>`

- **Preview validation + user messaging**
  - Added `ipfs.io` HEAD polling for uploaded `CID/filename` to detect propagation.
  - Added explicit UX messaging that:
    - uploads are pinned for ~1 hour,
    - preview may take time to appear,
    - files over a few MB may take significantly longer.

- **Preview/render compatibility**
  - Reused existing attachment preview pipeline so uploaded IPFS files render like standard IPFS lookups without changing downstream compose/post rendering behavior.

```js
const r = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/ipfs`, { method: 'POST', body: fd });
const cid = extractUploadCid(await parseUploadResponse(r));
const canonical = `IPFS:${cid}/${sanitizeUploadFileName(file.name)}`;
S.composeAttachments.push(canonical);
await waitForIpfsIo(cid, file.name); // preview readiness check on ipfs.io
```

title: Fix thread icon on profile searches for liked posts
body:
The thread icon for liked posts was not displaying on the profile search page, while it correctly showed up in the global recent search. This occurs because the `GetPublicMessagesByAddress` endpoint used by profile searches returns a different structure (`ToAddress` / `ToAddresses`) instead of the `Output` object present in other endpoints. 

This PR updates `normalizeRoot` to correctly fallback to parsing `ToAddress`/`ToAddresses` when the `Output` structure is absent. It also updates `asRoot` to ensure the parsed `toAddrs` array is preserved when parsing from cache.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [10563124863522231962](https://jules.google.com/task/10563124863522231962) started by @embiimob*

title: fix: Thread icon not displaying for likes on profile feeds
body:
- Modified the `buildTweetEl` HTML template to conditionally unhide the thread button (`display: ''` instead of `none`) if `likedTxid` is extracted.
- This ensures the thread button always displays for likes regardless of whether asynchronous engagement metrics successfully fetch or execute `updateTweetCardUi`.

title: Display deterministic emoji and liked txid reference for likes
body:
This commit addresses the issue of "like" posts showing up as blank in the stream. It does this by extracting the salt value from the post (which is typically negative) and using it to deterministically pick a single emoji from an extensive, randomized list of valid emojis. Additionally, it extracts the first 20 characters of the liked transaction ID from the `toAddrs` array (by reversing the base58 check format) and renders a clickable "exploreTag" link within the post so that users can quickly navigate to the liked thread.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [8979373591450403101](https://jules.google.com/task/8979373591450403101) started by @embiimob*

title: Fix profile pin and links 3522893413427633469
fix: properly parse pinned tweets and profile @URN links
body:
Resolves issue where profile url links matching "pin" with a txid value didn't actually pin on the social feed but showed up on the profile links list. The update correctly removes "pin" keys from `linksHtml` and implements a block in `renderProfileWithPagination` that identifies pin URLs, fetches the root payload, matches it against the profile creator's address, and correctly renders it either as a direct tweet or a retweet reference at the top of the timeline. Furthermore, standard links with values starting with `@` now utilize the built-in `openUserProfile(URN)` internal navigator, fixing the broken redirect logic.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [3522893413427633469](https://jules.google.com/task/3522893413427633469) started by @embiimob*

title: Fix gif preview in gif picker
body:
The user reported that gif previews are not showing up when searching in the gif picker modal. The issue is due to `loadMoreGifSelector` improperly filtering out valid gif candidate URLs (specifically, those representing the base CID URL without a `.gif` extension) using `isGifToken`. I have removed the `filter` logic. Playwright tests pass and confirm previews are now successfully rendered.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [16727145552542197723](https://jules.google.com/task/16727145552542197723) started by @embiimob*

title: Fix GIF picker blank/black thumbnails by correcting tile image assignment and stabilizing load behavior
body: GIF search was returning many candidates, but most tiles rendered as empty/black placeholders and disappeared under gateway/load pressure, even though selecting a tile still attached a valid image. The picker needed both correct thumbnail binding and lower-pressure loading/fallback behavior. - **Thumbnail render correctness** - Fixed tile image assignment so each newly created `` always receives its URL. - Removed the guard that skipped assignment because `img.src` is already non-empty by default on DOM-created images. - **Picker rendering stability** - Kept GIF grid rendering append-only to avoid repeatedly tearing down/recreating existing tiles. - Prevented unnecessary mid-load re-renders that reset in-flight thumbnail loads. - **IPFS request pressure + fallback reliability** - Reduced GIF fetch batch size to limit concurrent API/gateway pressure. - Staggered thumbnail `src` assignment to spread requests over time. - Updated gateway rotation to public-working options and removed dead/auth-required entries. - Changed exhausted-fallback behavior to keep tile slots instead of removing nodes, avoiding “all disappear” UX. ```js // before setTimeout(()=>{ if (img && !img.src) img.src = it.url; }, relIdx * 200); // after setTimeout(()=>{ if (img) img.src = it.url; }, relIdx * 200); ```

title: Simplify claimed-profile status UI and fix “See official” navigation
body: Claimed/unofficial profiles were showing redundant status signals and the **See official** action could no-op depending on current view. This update keeps the warning emoji as the non-official indicator and makes the link reliably open/search the canonical `@handle` in Explore. - **Navigation fix** - Switched the `See official` action from a direct search call to the existing profile-navigation helper so it always: - switches to Explore, - syncs the search input, - executes the `@URN` lookup. - **Status UI cleanup** - Removed the red `Not official` badge from unofficial profiles. - Kept: - ⚠️ claim indicator for unofficial/claimed handles, - `See official` link, - `✓ Official` badge for verified canonical profiles. ```html ! Not official ${official ? `✓ Official` : ``} ```
